questions.question: Which national body is responsible for enforcing the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974?
Under the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974, an employer must provide a safe workplace and safe systems of work 'so far as is reasonably ___'. Which word completes the phrase?
Which regulations build on the 1974 Act and make carrying out risk assessments an explicit legal requirement?
options: The Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999
The Lifting Operations and Lifting Equipment Regulations

questions.question: In the HSE's five-step approach to risk assessment, what is the SECOND step?
options: Decide who might be harmed and how
Record your significant findings and act on them

questions.question: Which regulations require your employer to assess hazardous substances such as cleaning products and disinfectants and tell you how to use them safely?
Which regulations specifically cover the moving of people and heavy loads in care?
options: The Lifting Operations and Lifting Equipment Regulations

questions.question: Under LOLER, how often is a hoist and its sling usually given a thorough examination?
Which regulations require work equipment such as hoists, beds and wheelchairs to be suitable, maintained and used only by people who are trained?
options: The Lifting Operations and Lifting Equipment Regulations (LOLER)
The Provision and Use of Work Equipment Regulations (PUWER)

questions.question: A fire needs three things together in order to start or continue. Which of these is one of them?
Which piece of legislation makes your employer responsible for assessing fire risks and keeping people safe from fire?
options: The Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974

questions.question: What should be in place for a person who cannot escape a building unaided?
options: A fire extinguisher by their bed

questions.question: Under RIDDOR, within how long must an over-seven-day injury report be made to the HSE?
Who is normally responsible for making a RIDDOR report to the HSE?
options: The responsible person, usually the employer

questions.question: You find a hoist that is out of its test date. What is the correct action?
options: Keep using it carefully because you are busy
Leave it for the next shift to notice
Try to repair it yourself straight away
Take it out of use, label it clearly and report it

questions.question: COSHH stands for the Control of Substances Hazardous to Health Regulations.
Under LOLER, a hoist and its sling are usually given a thorough examination only once every twelve months.
If you smell gas in someone's home, you should avoid using light switches or naked flames and get fresh air in.
It is safe to combine different cleaning chemicals to save time, provided they are kept in labelled containers.
Which of the following are common hazards in care settings described in the material? (Select all that apply)
Which of the following should you do if you discover a fire? (Select all that apply)
options: Make sure the fire service has been called on 999
Go back inside to fetch belongings
Raise the alarm and warn people nearby
Help people evacuate by the nearest safe route

questions.question: Which of the following are safe ways to handle hazardous substances in care? (Select all that apply)
options: Keep substances in their original labelled containers
Read the label and COSHH information before use
Store products locked away from the people you support
Mix cleaning chemicals together to save time

questions.question: Which of the following count as work equipment covered by PUWER? (Select all that apply)
The Control of Substances Hazardous to Health Regulations are commonly known by the abbreviation ___.
Fire needs three things together: a source of heat, fuel to burn and ___ from the air.
A person who cannot escape a building unaided should have a Personal Emergency Evacuation Plan, known by the abbreviation ___.
